claim: In far-from-equilibrium systems, structural persistence requires maintenance capacity M to exceed perturbation amplitude δ (M > δ); both quantities are domain-specifically bounded by physical or biological constraints.
statement: In far-from-equilibrium systems, structural persistence requires maintenance capacity M to exceed perturbation amplitude δ (M > δ); both quantities are domain-specifically bounded by physical or biological constraints.
scope: DEPRECATED — M > δ is a sufficient condition in THROUGHPUT-class systems only. Fails as a universal necessary condition: BARRIER-class systems persist with M ≈ 0 via kinetic trapping; CRITICAL-class systems exhibit maximum structural complexity at M ≈ δ, not M >> δ. Surviving content absorbed into kernel-001 as Class B of the Stability Mechanism Trichotomy.
assumptions: The system is demonstrably far from thermodynamic equilibrium.
Maintenance capacity M and perturbation amplitude δ are formally definable in the target domain.
Physical or biological upper bounds on M and δ exist and are non-trivial.
formal_objects: M: maintenance capacity — domain instantiations: energy flux (thermodynamic), exchange interaction strength (physical ordering), autopoietic closure rate (biological), neural integration rate proxy (neural)

δ: perturbation amplitude — domain instantiations: thermal noise kT (thermodynamic), symmetry-breaking fluctuation amplitude (physical), pathogenic or entropic load (biological), sensory prediction error (neural)
Far-from-equilibrium constraint: δ > 0 and system is not at thermodynamic ground state
differentiators: DEPRECATED: M > δ is not a universal necessary condition for structural persistence. Three independent countermodel classes falsify universality.

Class A countermodel (BARRIER): Diamond (sp3 carbon lattice) persists indefinitely far from equilibrium (graphite is the ground state) with M ≈ 0 active throughput. Stability is provided by kinetic barrier E_a ≈ 7 eV per bond >> kT ≈ 0.025 eV. Kernel-000 predicts structural collapse (M ≈ 0 < δ = kT > 0); diamond is stable. FAIL.

Class B countermodel (CRITICAL): Self-organized criticality (Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld sandpile) exhibits maximum structural complexity at M ≈ δ — exactly the critical manifold kernel-000 calls unstable. M >> δ would quench the system into an ordered, less complex state. FAIL.

Class C countermodel (STOCHASTIC RESONANCE): Stochastic resonance regimes (Gammaitoni et al., Phys. Rep. 1998) demonstrate that δ > M can enhance structural organization and signal detection coherence. Kernel-000 predicts collapse; observation shows optimal structure at intermediate δ > M. FAIL.

Surviving content: THROUGHPUT-class systems (autopoietic organisms, Bénard convection, driven chemical oscillators) where M > δ is the operative stability condition. Absorbed into kernel-001 as Class B.
Derived from compression of ont-001..004; those claims remain valid within their respective stability classes under kernel-001.

break_tests.setup: Perfect crystal at 0K. δ → 0, M → 0. Is M > δ satisfied?
break_tests.expected: If universal without equilibrium exclusion, crystal must satisfy M > δ for persistence.
break_tests.observed: Crystal at 0K is at thermodynamic equilibrium (δ ≈ 0). Scope explicitly excludes equilibrium systems. Claim does not apply.
break_tests.verdict: PASS — equilibrium exclusion correctly handles this case without falsifying the claim.

break_tests.setup: Living cell treated with metabolic inhibitor eliminating ATP production (M → 0) in environment with pathogenic load δ > 0.
break_tests.expected: If M > δ fails, structural persistence (life) terminates.
break_tests.observed: Cell dies. Biological structure collapses when maintenance capacity is eliminated under non-zero perturbation.
break_tests.verdict: PASS — claim confirmed for THROUGHPUT-class biological systems.

break_tests.setup: Ferromagnet: above T_c (δ_thermal > M_exchange → disordered); below T_c (M_exchange > δ_thermal → ordered).
break_tests.expected: Ordered structure appears only when M > δ.
break_tests.observed: Above T_c: disordered symmetric state (M < δ). Below T_c: broken-symmetry ordered state (M > δ). Transition at M = δ is T_c.
break_tests.verdict: PASS — claim confirmed for THROUGHPUT-class physical ordering transition.

break_tests.setup: Diamond at room temperature. δ = kT ≈ 0.025 eV (thermal noise). Active maintenance M ≈ 0 (no energy throughput sustaining the sp3 lattice against graphitization).
break_tests.expected: If M > δ is universally necessary, diamond (M ≈ 0 < δ = kT > 0) should structurally collapse toward graphite.
break_tests.observed: Diamond is indefinitely stable at ambient conditions. Graphite is the thermodynamic ground state (ΔG ≈ −2.9 kJ/mol favoring graphite), confirming diamond is far from equilibrium. Stability mechanism is kinetic barrier E_a ≈ 7 eV per C–C bond >> kT, not active throughput M. Kernel-000's M > δ condition is not satisfied and is not the operative mechanism.
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — kernel-000 is falsified for BARRIER-class systems. M > δ is not a universal necessary condition for structural persistence.

break_tests.setup: Bak-Tang-Wiesenfeld sandpile driven to the self-organized critical state. M ≈ δ (input flux ≈ dissipation rate at criticality).
break_tests.expected: If M > δ is necessary for structural persistence, the sandpile at M ≈ δ should collapse or be quenched into an ordered state without complex structure.
break_tests.observed: SOC sandpile at M ≈ δ exhibits maximum structural complexity: scale-free avalanche distributions, long-range correlations, 1/f noise spectrum. This IS the maximally structured state. Driving M >> δ would quench the system into subcritical, less structured behavior.
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — kernel-000 inverts the stability condition for CRITICAL-class systems. Maximum structure occurs at M ≈ δ, not M >> δ.

break_tests.setup: Stochastic resonance system: subthreshold periodic signal (amplitude M_signal < δ_noise). Kernel-000 predicts structural signal detection fails.
break_tests.expected: If M > δ is universally necessary, noise-dominated regimes (δ > M_signal) should preclude structured coherent detection.
break_tests.observed: At optimal intermediate noise δ > M_signal, stochastic resonance maximizes signal-to-noise ratio and coherent structural response. The δ > M regime generates structured output that the noiseless case cannot. (Gammaitoni et al., Phys. Rep. 1998.)
break_tests.verdict: FAIL — kernel-000 excludes the stochastic resonance stability class. δ > M can be constructive, not destructive.

falsifiers: A far-from-equilibrium system demonstrably persisting structurally with M < δ by domain-specific metrics.
A domain where M and δ cannot be formally operationalized, requiring scope reduction rather than falsification.
